ddls:
CREATE TABLE "instructions" (
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instruction */,
  "instruction_name" TEXT /* Name of the instruction */,
  "instruction_type" TEXT /* Type of the instruction (e.g., arithmetic, control flow, memory access) */,
  "latency" REAL /* Latency of the instruction */,
  "reciprocal_throughput" REAL /* Reciprocal throughput of the instruction */,
  "mnemonic" TEXT /* Mnemonic of the instruction */,
  "operand_count" INTEGER /* Number of operands the instruction accepts */,
  "operand_types" TEXT /* Types of operands the instruction accepts (e.g., register, immediate) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instruction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instructions_instruction_type FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_type") REFERENCES instruction_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instruction type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the instruction type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the instruction type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cpu_architectures" (
  "architecture_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each CPU architecture */,
  "architecture_name" TEXT /* Name of the CPU architecture */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the CPU architecture */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the CPU architecture */,
  "clock_speed" REAL /* Clock speed of the CPU architecture */,
  "num_cores" INTEGER /* Number of cores in the CPU architecture */,
  "num_threads" INTEGER /* Number of threads supported by the CPU architecture */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("architecture_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_set" (
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction */,
  "architecture_id" INTEGER /* ID of the CPU architecture the instruction belongs to */,
  "extension_support" INTEGER /* Indicates if the instruction is supported by extensions (e.g., SSE, AVX) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instruction_id", "architecture_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fu_utilization" (
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction */,
  "fu_type" TEXT /* Type of the functional unit (e.g., ALU, AGU, FP) */,
  "utilization" REAL /* Utilization of the functional unit for the instruction */,
  "access_pattern" TEXT /* Access pattern of the functional unit (e.g., sequential, random) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instruction_id", "fu_type")
)

CREATE TABLE "fu_types" (
  "fu_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each functional unit type */,
  "fu_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the functional unit type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the functional unit type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fu_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, researcher, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department FOREIGN KEY ("department") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the instruction */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the instruction was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, analyze) */,
  "query_params" TEXT /* Query parameters used to access the instruction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_performance" (
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction */,
  "performance_metric" TEXT /* Performance metric (e.g., latency, throughput) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the performance metric (e.g., cycles, instructions per second) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instruction_id", "performance_metric")
)

CREATE TABLE "cpu_performance" (
  "architecture_id" INTEGER /* ID of the CPU architecture */,
  "performance_metric" TEXT /* Performance metric (e.g., IPC, clock speed) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the performance metric (e.g., instructions per cycle, GHz) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("architecture_id", "performance_metric")
)

CREATE TABLE "fu_performance" (
  "fu_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the functional unit type */,
  "performance_metric" TEXT /* Performance metric (e.g., utilization, throughput) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the performance metric (e.g., percentage, operations per second) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fu_type_id", "performance_metric")
)

CREATE TABLE "optimization_history" (
  "optimization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each optimization */,
  "architecture_id" INTEGER /* ID of the CPU architecture the optimization was made to */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the optimization */,
  "optimization_date" TEXT /* Date the optimization was made */,
  "optimization_type" TEXT /* Type of optimization (e.g., pipeline optimization, cache optimization) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("optimization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_optimization_history_architecture_id FOREIGN KEY ("architecture_id") REFERENCES cpu_architectures ("architecture_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "optimization_results" (
  "optimization_id" INTEGER /* ID of the optimization */,
  "performance_metric" TEXT /* Performance metric (e.g., IPC, clock speed) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric after the optimization */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the performance metric (e.g., instructions per cycle, GHz) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("optimization_id", "performance_metric")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_departments" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "department_id")
)
